connectorUiConfig.title: Valence Security
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Valence Security](https://valencesecurity.com/) connector enables ingestion of Valence SaaS security alerts and audit logs into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF) Push pattern. Valence's unified SSPM platform detects SaaS misconfigurations, identity risks, and data exposure across Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Salesforce, GitHub, Slack, and more. This connector streams `ValenceAlerts_CL` (security alerts) and `ValenceAuditLogs_CL` (platform audit trail) into Microsoft Sentinel for analysis, alerting, and visualization.

sampleQueries.query:
ValenceAlerts_CL
 | where severity == "Critical"
 | sort by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: ValenceAlerts_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ValenceAlerts_CL
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: ValenceAuditLogs_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
ValenceAuditLogs_CL
 | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
 | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
union ValenceAlerts_CL, ValenceAuditLogs_CL
 | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
 | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(7d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Microsoft Entra
customs.description: Permission to create an app registration in Microsoft Entra ID. Typically requires the Entra ID Application Developer role or higher.

customs.name: Microsoft Azure
customs.description: Permission to assign the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on the data collection rule (DCR). Typically requires the Azure RBAC Owner or User Access Administrator role.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Create ARM Resources and Provide the Required Permissions
instructionSteps.description:
Clicking **Deploy push connector resources** will create the Data Collection Endpoint (DCE), Data Collection Rule (DCR), and the custom log tables (`ValenceAlerts_CL` and `ValenceAuditLogs_CL`). It will also create a Microsoft Entra app registration with a client secret and grant it the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on the DCR, enabling Valence to push data securely via the Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion API.

This replaces the legacy Workspace ID and Primary Key configuration — **no shared keys are used**.

parameters.content:
#### Automated deployment of Azure resources
Clicking **Deploy push connector resources** creates the DCE, DCR, and custom log tables. An Entra app registration with a client secret is also created and granted the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on the DCR.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Configure the Valence Platform
instructionSteps.description: Provide the values below to your Valence Security administrator. In the Valence Platform, navigate to **Settings → Configuration → SIEM Integration → Microsoft Sentinel** and enter these values to enable the CCF Push integration.

parameters.label: Entra Application (Client) ID
parameters.placeholder: Deploy push connector to get the Application ID

parameters.label: Entra Application Secret
parameters.placeholder: Deploy push connector to get the Application Secret

parameters.label: Data Collection Endpoint URI (LogIngestionURL)
parameters.placeholder: Deploy push connector to get the DCE URI

parameters.label: DCR Immutable ID
parameters.placeholder: Deploy push connector to get the DCR Immutable ID
